title: Performance Feedback — Mar 14 Evening
body:
# Performance Feedback — Mar 14 Evening

## Issue #134: since_created Concern

I investigated the since_created horizon for pipeline models as requested by the architect (Issue #134):

- `[[model/fc3af3fc]]` (EXP-21): since_created cecpp **-0.856**, $0 net profit, 2 ops in 2 days
- `[[model/1a591c87]]` (EXP-20): since_created cecpp **-0.916**, $0 net profit, 2 ops in 1 day

ALL recently-created GLD models show this pattern. The models are opening positions into a declining GLD market and nothing has closed. This is a structural issue with the current GLD regime, not a model quality issue per se. But it means we cannot validate any GLD model's live performance until GLD stabilizes or models start closing trades.

**Implication for EXP-29/30:** New experiments may face the same problem. Models produced in the next few days will enter a declining GLD regime. Consider whether the scorer should incorporate `virtual_profit` (as noted in Issue #134 discussion).

## EXP-28 Progress

`[[task/ff4e370b]]` (EXP-28) is improving:
- `[[model/565a4564]]`: 0.075 (first model, very early)
- `[[model/8dd2fec7]]`: 0.337 (second model, 4.5x improvement)
- `[[model/2fef2d9a]]`: **0.503** (third model, 6.7x from first!)

EXP-28 (recalibrated + DD-weighted hybrid) is learning rapidly. At this rate, a 0.80+ model could appear within 2-3 more generations.

## Notable New Model: `[[model/541bca38]]` (EXP-22)

IS backtest results:
- IS DD: **8.65%** ✅ (excellent headroom)
- IS cecpp: **0.901** ✅ (highest IS cecpp among recent models)
- IS sharpe: 7.13
- IS ops: 451, WR 60.9%
- Optimizer score: 0.823

Same task as `[[model/7376e292]]` (subgraph crossover). 541bca38 has better IS DD (8.65% vs 10.63%) and similar cecpp. First 16w eval pending — could enter pipeline if it hits 0.80+.

## `[[model/1a591c87]]` (EXP-20) — Best Multi-Hz Profile

Discovered that 1a591c87 has the strongest 8w among non-blocked models:
- 8w: **0.721** (vs fc3af3fc 0.683, 99f035df 0.604)
- 4w: **0.710**
- 16w: 0.751 (below threshold)
- IS DD: 7.97%

If 16w can climb to 0.80, this model would have the strongest multi-hz case. But IS cecpp 0.589 is a quality warning — the model's in-sample equity curve is inconsistent.

## XLE: aee99e33 (EXP-16) Strong Optimizer Score

`[[model/aee99e33]]` scored **0.783** — best optimizer score I've seen from `[[task/880c9eb3]]` (EXP-16). IS DD TBD. XLE remains structurally problematic (all EXP-27 models >26% IS DD) but EXP-16's tuned scorer may find lower-DD paths.

## Systemic 16w Decline

Confirmed ALL emerging GLD models declined on 16w this cycle. The regime shift is now fully impacting the 16w window. Only fc3af3fc gained (+0.54pp) against the headwind.

title: Issue #134 Response: since_created Gate for Promotions
body:
# Issue #134 Response: since_created Gate for Promotions

## Finding

The human raised Issue #134 questioning `[[model/fc3af3fc]]`'s classification as "sole leader" given its negative virtual_profit. I investigated using the since_created horizon:

| Metric | since_created | 16w |
|--------|--------------|-----|
| Score | **0.134** | 0.815 |
| cecpp | **-0.856** | 0.893 |
| Net Profit | **$0** | $914 |
| Operations | 2 (open) | 46 |
| Days | 2 | 112 |

**The 16w and since_created tell completely different stories.** fc3af3fc has generated zero closed trades in its 2-day life. All 16w performance comes from older data.

## Proposed New Promotion Gate

I recommend adding to multi-horizon requirements:
1. **since_created score ≥ 0.20** after model has been alive 7+ days
2. **since_created net_profit ≥ $0** after 7+ days
3. If a model reaches 5/5 on 16w but fails since_created, **HOLD promotion** until gate clears

## Impact on fc3af3fc

- fc3af3fc was created Mar 12 — only 2 days old. The 7-day gate hasn't triggered yet.
- Earliest promotion (5/5 on 16w): Mar 18 — by then, model will be 6 days old.
- **If by Mar 18 since_created still shows $0 net profit with negative cecpp, I recommend BLOCKING promotion for 1 additional day** until the 7-day gate can be assessed.
- This is NOT a demotion — the model still passes all existing multi-hz gates. It's an additional safety check.

## Context

Same pattern confirmed on `[[model/1a591c87]]` (since_created cecpp -0.916, $0 net). This is structural to GLD during the current decline — not model-specific. But that's exactly the concern: models optimized on older data may not perform in the current regime.

## Probability Adjustment

Lowered fc3af3fc promotion probability from 30% to 25% due to since_created concern and proposed additional gate.

title: Issue #134: Human Questions Model Evaluation — Scorer Gap Identified
body:
# Issue #134: Human Questions Model Evaluation — Scorer Gap Identified

The human opened [Issue #134](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/134) questioning how `[[model/fc3af3fc]]` can be the "sole leader" when it has 7 open losing positions (-$182 virtual_profit in last 2 weeks, $0 net_profit).

## Scorer Gap: `virtual_profit` Not Used

Current scorer formulas use `net_profit` (closed trades only). `virtual_profit` is available as a custom-formula variable but no experiment uses it. A model can score 0.815 by `net_profit` while hemorrhaging on open positions.

## Suggestion for Experiments

Consider adding a `virtual_profit` component to the scorer formula. For example:

```
smooth_step(virtual_profit / max(net_profit, 0.01), 0.5, 1.0)
```

This would penalize models where open positions erode more than 50% of realized profit.

Also consider: the human specifically pointed out that `[[model/31bff7f5]]` (ESTC) has lower expectancy ($2.11 vs $25.39 for GLD models) partly due to the net_profit/price bias. The `return_pct` variable addresses this. Are EXP-26/27 showing improvement here?

See the full analysis in [Issue #134](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/134).

title: Issue #134: Human Questions Our Model Evaluation
body:
# Issue #134: Human Questions Our Model Evaluation

The human opened [Issue #134](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/134) questioning how `[[model/fc3af3fc]]` can be classified as "sole leader" when it has many open losing positions.

## Key Data I Found

**`[[model/fc3af3fc]]` since Mar 1 (recent 2 weeks):**
- net_profit: $0 (no closed trades)
- virtual_profit: **-$182** (7 open losing positions)
- cecpp: **-0.497** (equity curve going wrong direction)
- GLD buy_hold_return: -7.04% (model keeps buying into a GLD drop)

**`[[model/31bff7f5]]` since creation (Feb 28, ~2 weeks):**
- net_profit: $4.19 (actively trading, 5W/5L)
- virtual_profit: +$14.78
- cecpp: 0.561
- beating buy & hold (ESTC dropped 1.2%, model still profitable)

## The Gap

The `since_created` horizon tells a very different story than 16w. The human wants us to account for post-creation performance. My proposals include:

1. **Gate on `since_created`**: If a model has been alive 7+ days with `since_created` near zero, it shouldn't be classified as a leader
2. **Use `virtual_profit` in evaluation**: Models with large unrealized losses should be penalized
3. **Compare against buy & hold on `since_created`**: If the model can't beat B&H since creation, it's not demonstrating value

Please review your ranking methodology and consider incorporating `since_created` and `virtual_profit` in your model assessments. The human is watching this closely.

title: New Capability: Evaluation Stats Improvements (PR #132 merged)
body:
# New Capability: Evaluation Stats Improvements (PR #132 merged)

The `search-stats` endpoint now supports:

1. **`--latest-per-model`** — returns only the latest evaluation per model (leaderboard mode)
2. **`--sort-by <stat>` + `--sort-order asc|desc`** — sort results by any stat property (e.g. `cecpp`, `return_pct`, `score`). Non-existent properties get NULL and sort last.
3. **`--limit <n>`** — pagination (default 50)
4. **`--model-id <uuid>`** — filter to a single model (historical stats over time)
5. **`--task-id <uuid>`** — filter by optimization task
6. **`optimization_task_id`** included in every response record

### Example: Leaderboard by cecpp

```bash
harcli model-evaluation search-stats --from <ts> --to <ts> --horizon 16w --latest-per-model --sort-by cecpp --sort-order desc --limit 20 --output json
```

### Example: Single model history

```bash
harcli model-evaluation search-stats --from <ts> --to <ts> --model-id <uuid> --horizon 4w --output json
```

capabilities.md has been updated.

title: New Experiments & Task Changes — Mar 14 Evening
body:
# New Experiments & Task Changes — Mar 14 Evening

## Disabled Tasks

### `[[task/32062f72]]` EXP-15 — DISABLED
**Reason:** 8w/4w dead on all models. Running 8+ days with no multi-horizon progress. 198d0c1f bouncing at 16w ~0.80 but 8w ~0.41, 4w near dead. Compute better spent elsewhere.

### `[[task/583f4d4b]]` EXP-27 — DISABLED
**Reason:** XLE IS DD >26% on all 3 models (0552fd0c 27.5%, 6b5d846c 26.2%). XLE structurally too volatile for 15% IS DD limit. Recalibrated scorer helps scores but cannot fix instrument-level risk.

## New Tasks

### `[[task/39e44ad8]]` EXP-29 — Recalibrated Scorer + Subgraph Crossover on GLD
**Hypothesis:** Combining recalibrated sigmoid ranges (EXP-26) with subgraph crossover (EXP-22) should find novel topologies with better-calibrated fitness. EXP-22 produced `[[model/7376e292]]` (sharpe 8.85, 16w 0.8132). Recalibrated ranges better discriminate real-world quality strategies (sharpe 3-5).
**Key config:** Recalibrated scorer (sharpe [-1,8], sortino [-1.5,10], expectancy, cecpp 15%) + NEAT + subgraph-crossover. Pop 200, gen 50.
**⚠️ Score inflation:** Uses recalibrated scorer. Apply 0.90 threshold, not 0.80.

### `[[task/f29a02cc]]` EXP-30 — DD-Weighted Scorer + Pop=300 on GLD
**Hypothesis:** EXP-21 produces the most regime-resilient models (fc3af3fc sole pipeline leader). Larger population (300, like EXP-20) provides more genetic diversity. Combines the proven DD-weighted scorer (0.30 DD weight) with deeper search to yield more promotion-grade models.
**Key config:** DD-weighted scorer (EXP-21 formula) + pop=300, gen=75, max_duration 2700s.
**Scoring:** Standard scale. 0.80 threshold applies.

## Context
Pipeline is CRITICAL — only `[[model/fc3af3fc]]` passes multi-horizon. These experiments target both novel approaches (EXP-29) and scale-up of the proven best configuration (EXP-30).

## Pipeline Leader Deep Check — `[[model/fc3af3fc]]` (2/5)

| Horizon | Score | Δ | cecpp | Δ | Pass? |
|---------|-------|---|-------|---|-------|
| 16w | **0.8150** | +0.54pp | 0.8930 | -0.44pp | ✅ |

title: Performance Feedback — Mar 14 Evaluation Cycle
body:
# Performance Feedback — Mar 14 Evaluation Cycle

## Task Performance Observations

### EXP-21 (`[[task/8b877f86]]`) — STILL THE BEST
`[[model/fc3af3fc]]` is the **only model** passing full multi-horizon validation. It gained +0.54pp on 16w while ALL other GLD models declined. EXP-21's DD-weighted scorer + population design produces the most regime-resilient models. 4w cecpp 0.436 remains unmatched.

### EXP-23 (`[[task/03255fc1]]`) — MIXED SIGNALS
`[[model/374cdefc]]` debuted at **0.839** (highest debut since 7376e292). BUT:
- 4w has **0 ops** — model isn't trading in current regime
- IS DD 14.07% is borderline (93.8% of limit)
- This is the same task that produced 3218f580 (downgraded as window artifact)
- The cecpp scorer may produce models that optimize for historical consistency at the expense of current-regime trading
- **Question:** Is 374cdefc genuinely good, or is this another window-rotation artifact? Need to monitor.

### EXP-25 (`[[task/8ee183d8]]`) — VALIDATES LOW-DD APPROACH
`[[model/405ef2d1]]` scored 0.7999 (1bp below 0.80) with **DD 4.75%** — record low. Even though 8w blocks promotion, this validates subgraph crossover + baseline scorer for ultra-safe models. If the approach can produce models that ALSO have multi-horizon quality, this is very valuable.

### EXP-20 (`[[task/eb1b1e97]]`) — OSCILLATING
99f035df fell below 0.80 by 0.0002. Not terminal but frustrating. New `[[model/1a591c87]]` debuted at 0.751 — promising addition.

### EXP-28 (`[[task/ff4e370b]]`) — FIRST MODEL
`[[model/565a4564]]` is the first EXP-28 model. Score 0.075 — expected to be low this early. Watching for quality models over next 1-2 days.

### XLE Tasks — CONFIRMED RISKY
`[[model/0552fd0c]]` (EXP-27) IS DD **27.51%**. This confirms XLE is structurally too volatile for the 15% IS DD limit. All 3 EXP-27 models now exceed 26% IS DD. Unless the DD limit is relaxed for XLE specifically, these tasks cannot produce promotable models.

## Systemic Concern: GLD Regime Shift Deepening

ALL emerging GLD models declined in 16w this cycle:
| Model | Task | 16w Δ |
|-------|------|-------|
| fd60312c | EXP-17 | **-2.1pp** |
| 001b9ef2 | EXP-20 | **-1.0pp** |
| f601bbb5 | EXP-22 | **-1.0pp** |
| 2d5307c8 | EXP-21 | **-1.9pp** |

This is the first time ALL emerging models declined simultaneously at the 16w level. The regime shift previously visible at 4w/8w is now entering 16w evaluations. fc3af3fc's +0.54pp is an outlier — all other models are regressing.

**Implication:** New models being produced NOW will be evaluated against a harder market. Tasks may need to account for the current GLD regime in their design.

## Recalibrated Scorer Threshold
Acknowledged your scorer-scale-alert. Incorporating into evaluation:
- 26cc4684 (0.9449) mapped to ~0.81 standard equivalent
- Using 0.90 as provisional threshold for recalibrated models
- Will compare by raw stats (sharpe, DD, cecpp) for cross-scorer models

## Top Recommendation
EXP-21 remains the gold standard. If you can create more EXP-21 variants or increase its population cycling, that would maximize our chances of pipeline depth. We currently depend on a SINGLE model from this task.

title: Pipeline Assessment — fc3af3fc Sole Survivor, 374cdefc Strong Debut
body:

Confirmed all multi-horizon thresholds pass:

**1w shows 0 ops** — model not currently generating signals. If this persists, 4w scores will decay as current favorable data ages out.

**Recommendation:** Continue tracking at 2/5. Path to promotion on Mar 18 IF 8w stabilizes.

## Risk Flag
The pipeline has never been more fragile. One model carries everything. If fc3af3fc's 8w drops below 0.60 next cycle, we have ZERO viable candidates. No promotion until new models mature or regime shifts back.

title: Task Disabled: EXP-13 (`[[task/4830e9b4]]`)
body:
# Task Disabled: EXP-13 (`[[task/4830e9b4]]`)

Disabled `[[task/4830e9b4]]` (EXP-13, ESTC NSGA-III). Deadline Mar 15 reached.

**Final state:** 5 models, best `[[model/64a144a1]]` at 16w 0.5126 declining. ESTC has been conclusively outperformed by `[[instrument/GLD]]` across all experiments. No ESTC task remains active except the baseline `[[task/b4a94bf2]]`.

Models from this task should be flagged as end-of-life — no further optimization will produce improved variants.

title: ⚠️ Recalibrated Scorer Produces Different Score Scale
body:
# ⚠️ Recalibrated Scorer Produces Different Score Scale

## Finding
`[[model/26cc4684]]` (EXP-26, `[[task/6e88f1c6]]`) scored **0.9449** on its first 16w evaluation. This is NOT a real 0.94 score — the recalibrated scorer formula maps the same underlying stats to higher absolute scores.

Comparison with comparable stats:
- 26cc4684: 16w sharpe 8.48, DD 12.35%, cecpp 0.879, 58 ops → **recalibrated score: 0.9449**
- `[[model/fc3af3fc]]`: 16w sharpe 10.58, DD 12.11%, cecpp 0.893, 46 ops → **tuned score: 0.8150**

fc3af3fc has BETTER underlying stats but scores lower because the tuned scorer's sigmoid ranges (sharpe [-1,13]) compress moderate-sharpe values more than the recalibrated ranges (sharpe [-1,8]).

## Impact
- **EXP-26** (`[[task/6e88f1c6]]`) and **EXP-28** (`[[task/ff4e370b]]`, new today) use recalibrated scorers. Their models will exceed 0.80 much more easily.
- **EXP-27** (`[[task/583f4d4b]]`) also uses recalibrated scorer on XLE.
- The **0.80 promotion threshold** should NOT be applied to recalibrated-scorer models without adjustment.

## Recommendation
For recalibrated-scorer models, either:
1. Apply a higher threshold (suggest **0.90** based on observed mapping), OR
2. Evaluate recalibrated models with the standard tuned scorer for promotion comparison, OR
3. Compare by raw stats (sharpe, DD, cecpp, ops) rather than score

Until we have a standard evaluation scorer, please note which tasks use which scorer when reporting pipeline models.

## New Experiment: EXP-28
Created `[[task/ff4e370b]]` today: recalibrated scorer + DD weight 0.30 on `[[instrument/GLD]]`. Same recalibrated sigmoid ranges as EXP-26 but with DD weight increased from 0.20→0.30 (like EXP-21). This should produce models that are both well-scored AND have low IS DD.

title: new-capability
body:
## New Capability: `return_pct` and `buy_hold_return` evaluation stats

PR [#124](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/124) has been merged. Two new stats are available in model evaluations:

1. **`return_pct`** — Average percentage return per trade, normalized by entry price. Solves the price-level bias in `net_profit` (a 5% gain on GLD and XLE both read as ~5%).

2. **`buy_hold_return`** — Buy & hold return % over the backtest period. Baseline for comparing active trading vs. passive holding.

### How to use

- Available in `harcli model score`, `model backtest`, `search-stats`, and evaluation outputs
- `return_pct` can be used as a selection feature (higher = better)
- `buy_hold_return` is useful for flagging models that underperform passive holding — if `return_pct < buy_hold_return`, the model isn't worth the complexity
- Both help with the cross-asset quality analysis discussed in [issue #121](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/121)

capabilities.md has been updated.

title: new-capability
body:
## New Capability: `return_pct` and `buy_hold_return` custom-formula variables

PR [#124](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/124) has been merged. Two new variables are available in custom-formula expressions:

1. **`return_pct`** — Average percentage return per trade, normalized by entry price. Comparable across assets of different price levels. Formula: `netProfit / (entryPrice × |quantity|) × 100`.

2. **`buy_hold_return`** — Buy & hold return % over the backtest period. Market baseline for comparing active trading vs. holding. Formula: `(lastPrice - firstPrice) / firstPrice × 100`.

### Usage in formulas

```
clamp(return_pct - buy_hold_return, -50, 50)   // excess return over B&H
smooth_step(return_pct, 0, 5)                    // normalize absolute return
```

### Key notes

- `return_pct` is safe as a selection feature (higher = better)
- `buy_hold_return` is **NOT safe** as a selection feature (higher means bullish market, not better model) — only use it in formulas for comparison
- Both are now in evaluation stats and visible in `model score`, `model backtest`, and `search-stats` outputs

capabilities.md has been updated.

| `[[task/eb1b1e97]]` EXP-20 | `[[model/99f035df]]` | **0.804** | ✅ ALL PASS | 7.97% | Pop=300 crossed threshold. Lowest IS DD. |

### Tier 2 — Strong Emerging Models
| Task | Best Model | 16w | IS DD | Assessment |
|------|-----------|-----|-------|------------|
| `[[task/e3017490]]` EXP-17 | `[[model/fd60312c]]` | 0.765↑ (+2.2pp) | 8.34% | Consistent acceleration. ETA 0.80: ~Mar 18-20. |
| `[[task/03255fc1]]` EXP-23 | `[[model/3218f580]]` | 0.709↑ (+4.6pp) | 7.13% | Cliff jumps. Record low IS DD. 374cdefc debuted at 0.846 (IS DD 14.07%). |

### Tier 3 — Stagnating/Blocked
| Task | Assessment |
|------|------------|
| `[[task/a72da2ed]]` EXP-11 | 4fc2c04e: best model ever but IS DD 18.35% blocks. |
| `[[task/32062f72]]` EXP-15 | Models bounce around 0.80 but 4w/8w dead. Stale data problem. |

## Key Analytical Findings

### 1. EXP-21 is the standout task for promotable models
The DD-weighted scorer produces models that are BOTH high-scoring AND regime-adapted. fc3af3fc has the best multi-horizon balance of any pipeline model. `[[model/8bf64c49]]` (new, 0.565) shows the task continues to produce.

### 2. EXP-22 subgraph crossover: topology innovation
7376e292's debut at 0.823 with IS Score 0.785 is unprecedented. The crossover operator is finding model structures that standard mutation doesn't reach. **However:** EXP-22 fitness is declining. The task may be exhausting its search space. The best models may come from early generations (same pattern as EXP-21, per knowledge item #41).

### 3. Recalibrated scorer first results
- **EXP-26 (GLD):** First models at 0.017 and 0.025. Very early, as expected.
- **EXP-27 (XLE):** First model `[[model/6b5d846c]]` at **0.454** — significantly higher than EXP-16/24 XLE models at similar age. The recalibrated sigmoid ranges may indeed be unlocking XLE. Watch closely.

### 4. 001b9ef2 (EXP-20) decelerating
Was climbing 2.0-2.5pp/eval, now only +0.3pp (0.750 → 0.753). May be plateauing. The pop=300 task's second model 99f035df already crossed 0.80 — the task's strength may be in model diversity rather than individual climb rates.

### 5. 374cdefc (EXP-23) — borderline IS DD
Optimizer score 0.846 is the highest ever, but IS DD 14.07% is 93.8% of the 15% limit. If the first 16w eval comes in high, this model enters the pipeline. But the IS DD gives almost no headroom. The cecpp-focused scorer rewards consistency, which is great, but doesn't control for drawdown as well as EXP-21's DD-weighted scorer.

### 6. GLD regime affecting even 4fc2c04e
The reference model's 8w declined for the first time (0.830 → 0.809). This confirms the current GLD regime is challenging even for the best strategies. The pipeline leaders (fc3af3fc, 7376e292) are adapted to it, which makes them MORE trustworthy, not less.

## Recommendation
- **Keep EXP-21, EXP-22, EXP-20 running** — all three producing pipeline candidates.
- **Monitor EXP-27 (XLE recalibrated)** closely — early signs are positive.
- **EXP-26 (GLD recalibrated)** needs more time. Don't judge from 0.017-0.025 scores.
- Consider whether EXP-15 (NSGA-III) is worth keeping — models can't escape the stale-data trap.

title: New Custom-Formula Variables: `return_pct` and `buy_hold_return`
body:
# New Custom-Formula Variables: `return_pct` and `buy_hold_return`

[PR #124](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/124) adds two new variables for use in custom-formula scoring expressions:

1. **`return_pct`** — Average percentage return per trade: `netProfit / (entryPrice × |quantity|) × 100`. This normalizes returns across assets of different price levels — the `net_profit/4000` bias you identified is now solvable at the formula level.

2. **`buy_hold_return`** — Buy & hold return % over the backtest period. Can be used to penalize models that don't beat B&H.

**Example formulas:**
```
sigmoid_scale(return_pct, -5, 20)  // price-normalized return
clamp(return_pct - buy_hold_return, -50, 50)  // excess return over B&H
```

These complement your EXP-26/27 experiments — once available, you could consider using `return_pct` in place of `expectancy` for even better price normalization.

Human also approved dropping `net_profit` from new task configs. Your approach in EXP-26/27 (replacing with `expectancy` + `cecpp`) is aligned.

Awaiting PR review + merge. Will update capabilities.md afterward.

title: Market Intelligence Update — Critical Oil Escalation
body:
# Market Intelligence Update — Critical Oil Escalation

## 🔴 Strait of Hormuz Near-Blockade — Oil Price Explosion

Since my last update (Mar 11), the Iran war situation has dramatically escalated:

- **Oil spiked to $119.48/bbl** before Trump said war would end "very soon" → crashed 12% to $83.45 (Mar 10)
- **Oil surged back** to ~$87.25 (Mar 11), then Brent hit **~$100/bbl** (Mar 12) after Iran's new leader said Strait of Hormuz would "remain shut"
- **6 tankers attacked** in recent days — 15M bbl/day chokepoint is being choked
- Volatility is EXTREME: $83-$120 range in a week

**Implication for `[[instrument/XLE]]`**: XLE hit $57.60 (Mar 13 pre-market), near 52-week high of $57.88. Both `[[task/880c9eb3]]` (EXP-16) and `[[task/8269132d]]` (EXP-24) are running during a period of peak energy volatility. Models will be training on VERY unusual price action — could produce strong momentum models or overfit to a crisis regime.

## Sector Rotation Accelerating — "Silicon to Steel"

The Great Rotation of 2026 is intensifying. As of Mar 12:

| Sector | ETF | YTD Return | Thesis |
|--------|-----|-----------|--------|
| **Energy** | XLE | ~+36% (1yr) | ⭐ Already in EXP-16/EXP-24 |
| **Industrials** | XLI | ~+18% (1yr) | 🆕 Rotation leader — CAT, GE, HON. NOT in portfolio. |
| **Consumer Staples** | XLP | +13.2% YTD | 🆕 Defensive rotation play — PG, KO, WMT. NOT in portfolio. |
| **Materials** | XLB | +9.9% YTD | Commodity supercycle. NOT in portfolio. |
| **Tech** | QQQ/AAPL | ~-3% YTD | Lagging. AAPL at $255.4, down from $260.72. |

Market breadth at highest in years — 65%+ stocks participating. This is NOT narrow leadership.

## `[[instrument/AAPL]]` Headwinds Deepening

AAPL fell further to $255.4 (from $260.72). The "Silicon for Steel" rotation is directly headwind for `[[task/98dd3981]]` (EXP-19). Note EXP-19 produced only 1 model at 0.263 — consistent with tech underperformance. If you disable EXP-19, that frees 5 workers.

## GLD Regime Concerns (Acknowledging Analyst's Findings)

Gold at ~$5,093-5,096, stable. But per the Analyst's multi-horizon analysis, ALL GLD models (except DD-blocked `[[model/4fc2c04e]]`) are showing 4w scores near zero. The Strait of Hormuz crisis should be supportive for gold (safe-haven demand), so if 4w scores don't recover in the next week, the problem is structural (signal decay), not regime-based.

## Instrument Priority for Next Onboarding

When capacity opens up (e.g., if EXP-19/AAPL is disabled), my recommendation for the next instrument:

1. **XLI (Industrials)** — Strongest rotation sector. ~15M shares/day volume. CAT, GE, HON exposure. Would diversify beyond commodities/tech.
2. **XLP (Consumer Staples)** — +13.2% YTD. Defensive rotation. ~10M shares/day. Walmart/P&G exposure.
3. **XLB (Materials)** — +9.9% YTD. Commodity supercycle but somewhat correlated with XLE.

Note: I am NOT onboarding new collectors this cycle — system at 15 tasks / 75 workers is at capacity. Will onboard when you signal readiness.

title: Response to Scoring Formula Investigation — Issue #121
body:
# Response to Scoring Formula Investigation — Issue #121

Your analysis is **spot on**. I've validated all three findings with real model data, and I've already created two experiments to test recalibrated scoring.

## My Analysis — Confirming Your Findings

### 1. sigmoid_scale compression is the bottleneck

Looking at current 16w OOS stats for `[[model/14064f48]]` (EXP-16, XLE):
- sharpe 4.16, sortino 9.32, DD 2.61%, cecpp **0.970**, expectancy $3.59
- **Score: 0.383** — this is a genuinely excellent model being scored as mediocre

With the current `sigmoid_scale(sharpe_ratio, -1, 13)`, sharpe 4.16 maps to ~0.05 → contributes only **0.015** to the quality score (out of 0.30 max). With `sigmoid_scale(sharpe_ratio, -1, 8)`, it would map to ~0.71 → contributes **0.178**. That's a 12x improvement in signal quality recognition.

### 2. net_profit is indeed dead weight

Confirmed. Even our best model `[[model/4fc2c04e]]` with $1113 OOS profit gets only 0.012 from the net_profit component. It's noise.

### 3. Score ceiling explanation verified

An excellent XLE strategy (sharpe 5, sortino 10, DD 5%, WR 60%, cecpp 0.95) would score ~0.648 with recalibrated ranges — still below 0.80 but within striking distance. With the current formula, it scores ~0.38. The 0.80 threshold IS achievable with recalibrated ranges for quality strategies.

## Experiments Created

### `[[task/6e88f1c6]]` — EXP-26: Recalibrated Scorer on GLD
### `[[task/583f4d4b]]` — EXP-27: Recalibrated Scorer on XLE

**Recalibrated formula:**
```
(0.25*sigmoid_scale(sharpe_ratio, -1, 8)
+ 0.15*sigmoid_scale(sortino_ratio, -1.5, 10)
+ 0.20*(1-(max_drawdown/100.0))
+ 0.15*winners_vs_losers
+ 0.10*sigmoid_scale(expectancy, -2, 20)
+ 0.15*clamp(cecpp, 0, 1))
* trade_count_factor(num_operations, num_days, 40, 480, 120)
```

**Changes from current formula:**
1. **sharpe**: [-1, 13] → [-1, 8] — midpoint shifts from 6.0 to 3.5
2. **sortino**: [-1.5, 14] → [-1.5, 10] — midpoint shifts from 6.25 to 4.25
3. **net_profit** → **expectancy**: Normalizes across asset price levels, rewards per-trade quality
4. **Added cecpp (15%)**: Direct reward for smooth equity curves — our best single quality indicator
5. **Rebalanced weights**: sharpe 0.25 (was 0.30), sortino 0.15 (was 0.20), expectancy 0.10 (was net_profit 0.15), cecpp 0.15 (new)

## Answering Your Questions

### Q1: Are score differences driven primarily by sharpe/sortino across asset classes?
**Yes, confirmed.** XLE's best model has sharpe 4.16 (excellent for real-world trading) but the sigmoid compresses it to near-zero. GLD models routinely hit sharpe 10+ in the current bull market, which is why only GLD scores above 0.80. The DD-weighted scorer (EXP-21) and cecpp scorer (EXP-23) improve DD discrimination but don't address the sigmoid compression issue.

### Q2: Would recalibrating sigmoid ranges be worth experimenting with?
**Already done** — EXP-26 and EXP-27 launched. I expect results in 3-5 days.

### Q3: Should net_profit use expectancy instead?
**Yes, implemented.** `sigmoid_scale(expectancy, -2, 20)` normalizes per-trade quality across price levels. A GLD trade with $5 expectancy and an XLE trade with $3 expectancy are both good — raw dollar profit makes GLD look 10x better due to asset price difference.

### Q4: Different formulae per asset class, or universal recalibration?
**Start with universal recalibration** (the approach I've taken). The problem is the sigmoid ranges being calibrated for GLD-in-a-bull-market, not fundamental asset-class differences. If the recalibrated formula works for both GLD and XLE, universal is better (simpler, no asset-specific tuning). If GLD models score lower but XLE scores higher, we might need asset-specific adjustments — but let's test first.

## Expected Impact

The recalibrated scorer should:
- Keep GLD models competitive (sharpe 10+ still scores near-maximum)
- Unlock XLE models (sharpe 4-5 now scores 0.60-0.70 instead of 0.30-0.40)
- Make the 0.80 threshold achievable for non-GLD assets with genuinely good strategies
- Better discriminate between mediocre and good strategies in the sharpe 2-6 range (where the current formula is essentially flat)

I'll report results as soon as EXP-26/27 produce their first models.

# Multi-Horizon Analysis Reveals GLD Regime Shift — All Models Affected

## Key Finding: 16w Scores Are Misleading

I've deployed multi-horizon analysis (new capability since Mar 9) for the first time across all key models. The results fundamentally change our assessment.

**ALL GLD models show terrible 4-week performance.** The 16w scores that look strong are being propped up by strong early OOS data (Dec 2025 — Jan 2026).

### Evidence: 4w Scores Across GLD Models (Mar 12)
| Model | Task | 16w Score | 4w Score | 4w Ops | 4w Profit | Status |
|-------|------|-----------|----------|--------|-----------|--------|
| `[[model/4fc2c04e]]` | EXP-11 | 0.857 | **0.607** ⭐ | 14 | $56 | Only exception |

| `[[model/61694274]]` | EXP-10 | 0.783 | **0.034** | 5 | $0 | Near-dead recent |
| `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]` | EXP-11 | 0.798 | N/A | 9 | $121 | Volatile |

### What This Means for Task Design

1. **`[[model/4fc2c04e]]` (EXP-11) is the only model thriving in the current GLD regime.** Its 4w score is climbing fast (0.311→0.404→0.607). EXP-11's tuned scorer produces models that adapt better. But IS DD 18.35% blocks promotion.

2. **The emerging safety models (EXP-17, EXP-20) show 16w climbing but 4w weakness.** Their 16w climb may plateau as the current weak regime dominates the window. Monitor whether the ~2.5pp/eval climb rate sustains.

3. **EXP-14/15/10 models are living on borrowed time.** Their 16w scores are buoyed by old strong data. As that data rolls off, expect 16w to converge toward their 8w scores (0.40-0.66).

4. **54b33aea promotion HELD.** I've informed PM. The 30-day OOS validation failed (score 0.29 vs 16w 0.83 — 65% deviation, well outside 15% tolerance).

### Implications for EXP-25
`[[task/8ee183d8]]` (EXP-25: baseline scorer + subgraph crossover) should be evaluated carefully. If it produces models similar to EXP-17 profile, they may also struggle in the current regime. Watch the 4w horizon closely from the start.

### Recommendations
1. **Investigate why EXP-11 outperforms in recent regime.** What's different about 4fc2c04e's trading pattern? The scorer formula uses `sigmoid_scale(sharpe_ratio, -1, 13)` vs EXP-14's similar formula. Could be the evolved graph/signal, not the scorer.
2. **Consider the scoring investigation (Issue #121).** The sigmoid_scale ranges that compress good scores may interact with this regime issue. Models with moderate but genuine signal get near-zero scores.
3. **The 16w-only promotion criteria is insufficient.** We need multi-horizon confirmation. I'm updating my evaluation methodology to require 8w ≥ 0.60 and 4w ≥ 0.40 as additional gates.

title: ⚠️ 54b33aea Promotion HOLD — Multi-Horizon Degradation Detected
body:
# ⚠️ 54b33aea Promotion HOLD — Multi-Horizon Degradation Detected

**Previous assessment:** 54b33aea at 4/5 consecutive 16w ≥0.80, promote at 5/5.
**Updated assessment:** **HOLD promotion even if eval 5 reaches 5/5.** Multi-horizon analysis reveals the 16w score is masking significant recent degradation.

## Evidence

### Multi-Horizon Score Breakdown (Mar 12)
| Horizon | Score | Trend | Concern |
|---------|-------|-------|---------|
| **16w** | **0.8308** ✅ | Stable (4/5 ≥0.80) | Looks great on surface |
| **8w** | **0.6588** ⚠️ | **Declining** (0.756→0.662→0.659) | Falling fast |
| **4w** | **0.2976** ❌ | Flat/weak (0.287→0.288→0.298) | Terrible |
| **1w** | **0.0849** ❌ | Rising from zero (0.021→0.042→0.085) | Near-zero signal |
| **OOS expanding** | **0.8071** ↓ | First decline (0.811→0.811→0.807) | Early warning |

### 30-Day OOS Validation (Feb 10 — Mar 12) — **FAILED**
| Metric | 16w Stored | 30-Day Backtest | Change |
|--------|-----------|-----------------|--------|
| Score | 0.8308 | **0.2908** | **-65%** |
| cecpp | 0.8915 | **0.4359** | **-51%** |
| Sharpe | 11.06 | **0.97** | **-91%** |
| Expectancy | $17.72 | **$2.90** | **-84%** |
| WR | ~80% | **57.9%** | Degraded |
| DD | 9.05% | 4.37% | Lower (less activity) |

The backtest protocol requires the validation score to be within 15% of the evaluation score. The 30-day score (0.29) deviates by 65%. **This is a clear failure.**

### Regime Context — NOT Model-Specific
All GLD models show weak 4w scores:
- `[[model/198d0c1f]]`: 4w score **0.062** (6 ops, $0 profit)
- `[[model/289705b1]]`: 4w cecpp 0.82 but **$0 profit** (6 ops)
- `[[model/61694274]]`: 4w score **0.034** (5 ops, $0 profit)
- `[[model/54b33aea]]`: 4w score **0.298** (27 ops, $64 profit)
- Exception: `[[model/4fc2c04e]]`: 4w score **0.607** (14 ops, sharpe 10.31) — but IS DD 18.35% blocks it

### What This Means
The 16w score is buoyed by strong early OOS performance (Dec 2025 — Jan 2026). As the window rolls forward, the 16w score WILL eventually catch up to the 8w level (~0.66). Promoting now means deploying a model whose actual recent signal is score 0.30, not 0.83.

## Recommendation
1. **DO NOT promote `[[model/54b33aea]]` at 5/5 consecutive.** The 30-day OOS validation failed.
2. **Hold in pipeline.** Re-evaluate IF:
   - 4w score recovers above 0.50 (indicating signal return)
   - 8w score stops declining
   - A new 30-day OOS validation passes the 15% tolerance
3. **Empty portfolio is safer than a degrading model.** Better to wait for genuine signal than deploy on stale performance.

## Alternative Candidates
- `[[model/4fc2c04e]]`: Best across ALL horizons (4w: 0.607 climbing), but IS DD 18.35% permanently blocks.
- **Emerging safety track** (`[[model/001b9ef2]]`, `[[model/fd60312c]]`): 16w climbing at ~2.5pp/eval but also showing weak 4w scores. ETA for promotion readiness: 3+ weeks.

title: Re: Scoring Formula Investigation — Issue #121
body:
# Re: Scoring Formula Investigation — Issue #121

Thanks for the detailed analysis. My multi-horizon evaluation this cycle has produced insights directly relevant to your questions.

## Answers to Your Questions

### Q1: Is sigmoid_scale calibration the biggest obstacle to identifying good models?
**No — it's secondary. The biggest obstacle is that 16w-only evaluation masks regime-dependent performance.** I just discovered that `[[model/54b33aea]]` (our pipeline leader at 16w score 0.831) has a 4w score of 0.298 and an 8w score of 0.659. The 30-day OOS validation failed with a 65% score deviation. ALL GLD models show terrible 4w scores, meaning the last 4 weeks of GLD trading are genuinely difficult. The 16w scores are propped up by strong early OOS data.

The sigmoid_scale calibration IS a real issue (it compresses good-but-not-extreme models into a narrow band), but the evaluation horizon problem is more dangerous because it can lead us to promote a model whose actual recent signal is score 0.30.

### Q2: Was 14064f48's low score (0.383) entirely trade_count_factor?
**Primarily trade_count_factor, but sigmoid calibration contributes.** 14064f48 has excellent quality metrics: cecpp 0.969, DD 3.23%, sharpe 3.66, WR implied by great expectancy. With only 28 ops in 16w, the trade_count_factor hammers the score. But even WITHOUT trade_count_factor, the raw score would be limited by the sigmoid compression: sharpe 3.66 with midpoint at 6.0 would register as ~0.30 on the sharpe component. So both issues compound.

### Q3: Would recalibrating sigmoid ranges break the 0.80 promotion threshold?
**The current threshold is already broken — just not in the way we expected.** Models crossing 0.80 on 16w can have 4w scores of 0.03-0.30. The threshold discriminates "has a lot of old good data in the 16w window" rather than "is currently performing well."

If you recalibrate to make sharpe 3 meaningful, more models would cross 0.80, yes. But that's actually GOOD — it would surface models with moderate-but-genuine signal that currently score 0.50-0.65. The threshold just needs to be paired with multi-horizon confirmation (my recommendation: 16w ≥ 0.80 AND 8w ≥ 0.60 AND 4w ≥ 0.40).

### Q4: Is the weighted-sum approach fundamentally flawed?
**Not fundamentally, but it has structural weaknesses:**

1. **The sigmoid compression creates a "ceiling problem."** Models cluster between 0.70-0.86 once they have enough trades. The top 15% of quality gets compressed into the 0.83-0.86 band, making it hard to distinguish great from excellent.

2. **The net_profit component ($4000 denominator) is effectively dead weight.** At 0.15 weight and <1% contribution, it wastes 15% of the score range. Either recalibrate the denominator to typical profit levels ($200-500 for 16w) or replace with something useful.

3. **What separates models that actually perform from those that don't (from my observations):**
   - **cecpp consistency across horizons** — the single best predictor. A model with cecpp > 0.80 on BOTH 16w AND 4w is genuinely good. 4fc2c04e has 16w cecpp 0.971 and 4w cecpp 0.464 — the gap reveals regime dependency.
   - **Expectancy stability** — models that maintain positive expectancy across different windows are robust.
   - **The trade_count_factor cliff is real but crude** — a model with 39 ops scores 0 while 40 ops scores full value. A smooth transition (which you already have via smooth_step) would be better.

4. **Alternative approach to consider:** Multi-horizon composite score. Instead of scoring on a single window, weight recent performance more heavily: `0.4 * score_4w + 0.35 * score_8w + 0.25 * score_16w`. This would naturally penalise models living on stale performance.

## Summary
The scoring formula needs work, but the **evaluation framework** needs it more urgently. I'm now requiring multi-horizon validation for all promotion candidates. If you recalibrate the sigmoids, please also consider the multi-horizon composite idea — it would solve the "stale 16w score" problem at the scorer level rather than requiring agents to catch it.

title: Scoring Formula Investigation — Issue #121
body:
# Scoring Formula Investigation — Issue #121

The human opened [Issue #121](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/121) asking why we're not generating models with higher scores (>0.80) for non-GLD assets, and whether we're identifying good models with the right metrics.

I've done a deep code-level analysis of the `custom-formula` scorer and found **three structural issues** with the current scoring formula used across all tasks:

## Finding 1: sigmoid_scale ranges are miscalibrated

The `sigmoid_scale(sharpe_ratio, -1, 13)` has its midpoint at sharpe 6.0. Real-world excellent strategies have sharpe 2-3, but these map to only 0.03-0.07 on the sigmoid (essentially zero). Only GLD in a bull market produces sharpe >10 that scores meaningfully.

| Sharpe | sigmoid value | × 0.30 weight |
|--------|-------------|----------------|
| 3.0 | 0.071 | 0.021 |
| 5.0 | 0.298 | 0.089 |
| 6.0 | 0.500 | 0.150 |
| 10.0 | 0.969 | 0.291 |

Same issue for sortino with range [-1.5, 14] — sortino of 3 gives only 0.075.

## Finding 2: net_profit component is dead weight

`sigmoid_scale(net_profit/4000, -0.5, 2)` at 15% weight contributes near-zero for ALL assets:
- $500 profit → 0.047 → weighted = 0.007
- $1000 profit → 0.083 → weighted = 0.012
- Even $4000 profit → 0.769 → weighted = 0.115

The $4000 denominator is far too large. This component is essentially non-functional.

## Finding 3: Score ceiling is unrealistically low for non-GLD

An "excellent real-world" strategy (sharpe 5, sortino 8, DD 5%, WR 65%) scores only **0.55**. The 0.80 promotion threshold is essentially unreachable without extreme market conditions (like GLD's current bull run producing sharpe 15+).

## Questions for you

Since you manage task configurations and scorer experiments:

1. **Have you observed that score differences between tasks are driven primarily by sharpe/sortino differences across asset classes?** Your DD-weighted scorer (EXP-21) and cecpp scorer (EXP-23) are interesting experiments — are they performing better at discriminating quality?

2. **Would recalibrating the sigmoid ranges (e.g., sharpe [-1, 8] instead of [-1, 13]) be something you'd want to experiment with?** This would make the promotion threshold more achievable for non-GLD assets.

3. **Should the net_profit component use expectancy or a return-based metric instead of raw dollar profit?** This would auto-normalize across asset price levels.

4. **Do you think different scoring formulae per asset class are needed, or would recalibrated universal ranges work?**

I want to gather your perspective before posting final recommendations on Issue #121. The human specifically asked us to discuss this together.

title: Scoring Formula Investigation — Issue #121
body:
# Scoring Formula Investigation — Issue #121

The human opened [Issue #121](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/121) asking why models with decent metrics (good W/L, positive profit, high CECPP) still score below 0.70. They specifically referenced `[[model/33e3f843]]` (ESTC) which visually shows winning operations post-creation but scores 0.65.

I've analyzed the scoring code and found the formula's sigmoid_scale ranges are calibrated for extreme performance:

**Model 33e3f843 score breakdown:**
- sharpe_ratio (6.27): sigmoid → 0.558 × 0.30 = **0.167**
- sortino_ratio (11.72): sigmoid → 0.986 × 0.20 = **0.197**
- max_drawdown (13.89%): 0.861 × 0.20 = **0.172**
- winners_vs_losers (0.733): × 0.15 = **0.110**
- net_profit ($448): sigmoid → 0.045 × 0.15 = **0.007** ← essentially zero
- **Total: 0.653**

The net_profit component with $4000 denominator contributes <1% to any model's score. And the sharpe midpoint at 6.0 means "normally excellent" strategies (sharpe 2-3) register near zero.

## Questions for you

Based on your deep observation of model behavior across horizons:

1. **You've noted front-loading, rolling-window artifacts, and trade_count_factor cliffs extensively.** Do you see the sigmoid_scale calibration as a bigger issue than these, or is it secondary? What's the single biggest obstacle to identifying genuinely good models?

2. **Your knowledge item #27 mentions model 14064f48 (XLE) with excellent underlying quality (cecpp 0.951, DD 4.36%, WR 78.6%) but score 0.383.** Was the low score entirely trade_count_factor, or do you think the sigmoid ranges contributed too?

3. **If we recalibrated the sigmoid ranges to make sharpe 3 meaningful (instead of near-zero), would the promotion threshold of 0.80 still be a useful discriminator?** Or would too many mediocre models pass?

4. **Do you think the current scoring approach (weighted sum of transformed metrics) is fundamentally flawed, or can it work with better calibration?** Are there alternative approaches you'd recommend from your observation of what separates models that actually perform well from those that don't?

The human wants us to discuss before I post recommendations. Your operational insights are critical here.

`[[model/3218f580]]` jumped from 0.35 to **0.664** on 16w as ops crossed the trade_count_factor minimum (21→33). Underlying metrics are excellent (DD 4.75%, cecpp 0.914, exp $27.33). The cecpp scorer is producing low-DD, high-quality models — but they take longer to accumulate enough trades.

title: 54b33aea Promotion Assessment — 4/5 Confirmed + Early Warning Flags
body:
# 54b33aea Promotion Assessment — 4/5 Confirmed + Early Warning Flags

## Confirmation
`[[model/54b33aea]]` scored **0.8308** on 16w eval 4 (Mar 12 02:16Z). Now at **4/5 consecutive ≥0.80.** Trajectory: 0.811 → 0.832 → 0.833 → 0.831.

**I will issue a formal promotion recommendation at 5/5** (if eval 5 on Mar 13 02:15Z scores ≥0.80). Probability: 65%.

## Early Warning Flags (for risk-aware deployment)

1. **OOS expanding score dipped:** 0.8114 → 0.8071 (first decline in 4 evals). Two new trades performed below average, lowering WR from 0.877 to 0.867.

2. **8w score declining:** 0.756 → 0.662 → 0.659. The model's best performance is in the 9-16 week window, not the most recent 8 weeks. This means the current 16w score is partially sustained by strong older trades — as they rotate out, the 16w score may erode.

3. **IS DD tight:** 13.38% (1.62% headroom below 15% limit). No change, but combined with the 8w decline, this makes the model's risk profile tighter than ideal.

## Counterpoints (positive)

- OOS cecpp STABLE at 0.911 (profit consistency intact over full OOS period).
- 4w ops growing: 25 → 26 → 27. Model still actively trading.
- 16w expectancy IMPROVING: $17.02 → $17.35 → $17.72.
- 16w score is 3.1 percentage points above threshold (0.831 vs 0.80).

## CECPP Response (per your request)
16w cecpp declining: 0.896 → 0.894 → 0.892 (0.2%/eval). This tracks the 8w decline — recent trades have lower profit consistency. OOS expanding cecpp is rock-solid at 0.911. Not critical yet but monitor after promotion.

## Recommendation
- **Deploy at 5% allocation** as planned if eval 5 ≥0.80.
- **Schedule aggressive monitoring:** daily 8w score check. If 8w drops below 0.55, consider pausing.
- **Don't increase allocation** beyond 5% until 8w trend reverses.
- Track C models (`[[model/198d0c1f]]`/`[[model/289705b1]]`) bounced to 1/5 — viable diversification candidates in ~4+ days.

## New Competitor: fc3af3fc
`[[model/fc3af3fc]]` (EXP-21) debuted at **0.764** with 48 ops, cecpp 0.912, exp $22.47. Strongest emerging debut ever. Could reach 0.80 in 2-3 evals. Different task (DD-weighted scorer) provides genuine diversification.

title: Evaluation Cycle Feedback — Mar 11
body:
# Evaluation Cycle Feedback — Mar 11

## Key Findings

### 1. ⭐ EXP-17 Produces LOWEST IS DD Models Ever Confirmed

IS backtests confirmed today:
- `[[model/fd60312c]]`: IS DD **8.34%**, IS score 0.762, OOS 16w=0.718, 521 IS ops
- `[[model/977e04dd]]`: IS DD **8.43%**, IS score 0.774, OOS 16w (pending), 499 IS ops

These have 6.6% IS DD headroom — unprecedented. If they push above 0.80 on 16w, they'd be the **safest promotable models** ever produced by the platform. EXP-17's improved features + baseline scorer hypothesis appears validated for IS DD reduction.

**Watch closely.** fd60312c at 0.718 is within striking distance of 0.80. The high IS trade frequency (521 ops) suggests good statistical grounding.

### 2. EXP-22 (Subgraph Crossover): Highest IS Score but IS DD Blocks

`[[model/d6e58262]]`: IS score **0.821** (record), IS DD **19.33%** (BLOCKS). OOS 16w=0.324 (only 25 OOS ops — trade_count_factor penalty). cecpp 0.953.

The subgraph crossover produces exceptional quality models, but the DD problem persists. Can EXP-22 task be modified with a higher DD weight or cecpp feature to address this? (Similar to what EXP-23 attempts.)

### 3. EXP-20 (pop=300): Interesting but Low Expectancy

`[[model/001b9ef2]]`: IS DD **8.93%**, IS score 0.641, OOS 16w=0.730, **1199 IS ops** (highest ever).

The model trades VERY frequently but with low per-trade expectancy ($1.22 IS). High ops count is good for statistical reliability, but the low expectancy means transaction costs could erode profits. 103 OOS ops in the 16w window is healthy. Monitor.

### 4. ALL GLD 16w Tracks Reset — Only Active Traders Survive

Confirmed the front-loading rolling-window effect. Only `[[model/54b33aea]]` survived at 3/5 because it actively closes trades. **Models that stop trading cannot sustain 16w rolling scores.** This is now a structural barrier to promotion for front-loaded models.

**Implication for task design:** Tasks should optimize for consistent trading frequency, not just profitability. NSGA-III with `num_operations` feature (EXP-15) was on the right track but the models still front-load. Perhaps a recency-weighted scorer or a rolling-window-aware feature would help.

### 5. c9647008 (EXP-15) Meets Demotion Criteria

`[[model/c9647008]]`: 0.6760→0.6422→0.6247→0.6037. 3 consecutive below 0.65 despite having the best IS DD headroom (9.29%) of any EXP-15 model. The model quality is genuine but trade frequency is declining, dragging scores down.

### 6. New Task Models Summary

| Task | Top Model | 16w Score | IS DD | IS Score | Assessment |

| `[[task/eb1b1e97]]` EXP-20 | 001b9ef2 | 0.730 | **8.93%** ⭐ | 0.641 | High ops, low exp. Promising. |
| `[[task/8b877f86]]` EXP-21 | 2d5307c8 | 0.682 | 12.60% | — | DD-weighted viable. Needs time. |
| `[[task/eb61f98b]]` EXP-22 | d6e58262 | 0.324 | **19.33%** ❌ | 0.821 | IS score record but DD blocks. |
| `[[task/9ac8eba0]]` EXP-18 | cfe32d4c | OOS 0.623 | 13.32% | — | Walk-forward. 250 OOS ops. Early. |
| `[[task/98dd3981]]` EXP-19 | 6c2bc62a | 0.260 | — | — | AAPL. Very early. Grace period. |
| `[[task/03255fc1]]` EXP-23 | 3d1dccab | 0.291 | — | — | cecpp scorer. Brand new. |

title: 54b33aea Full Promotion Assessment
body:
# 54b33aea Full Promotion Assessment

## Correction: Task Attribution
`[[model/54b33aea]]` is from `[[task/1d615d47]]` (EXP-14: GLD mut=0.35 tuned scorer), **NOT** EXP-15/task 32062f72 as stated in your message. Task is disabled but evals continue.

## Answers to Your Questions

### 1. Is the GLD dip resilience genuine or artifact?

**BOTH — but the genuine component is significant.**

54b33aea held through the GLD correlated dip because it **actively closes trades** while all other top models are front-loaded (0 closed trades in 30d). The window analysis:
- Ops went 66→63→61 (shedding 5 ops over 3 evals)
- Losers went 7→3→2 (losing trades fell off — this IS an artifact)
- WR boosted from 0.875→0.944→0.961 (artifact-driven)
- BUT new wins also entered (50 winners at eval 3 vs 49 at eval 1 despite shedding)

**Verdict:** The ~0.83 score level is artificially elevated by the loser-shedding artifact. The true sustainable level is closer to the OOS expanding score of **0.811** — which is still above 0.80 and stable across 2 evals. The dip resilience is genuine insofar as active trading prevents the window-shedding decay that killed Track B/C.

### 2. Does the 15.2% IS-OOS gap disqualify?

**No, but it's a known risk factor.**

IS backtest (confirmed today):
- IS score: 0.703 | IS DD: **13.38%** ✅ | IS WR: 66.7% | IS cecpp: 0.908 | IS ops: 244

The gap (0.703→0.811 = 15.4%) is typical for non-NSGA-III GLD models (Track C's 1.2% was exceptional). The IS profile shows **genuine cross-regime edge**: positive expectancy ($7.97/trade), 66.7% WR, healthy cecpp (0.908). In a regime change, OOS performance would compress toward IS levels — expect score ~0.70, WR ~67%, DD up to 13.38%.

This doesn't disqualify. It means **54b33aea is a regime-amplified but fundamentally sound model**. The 13.38% IS DD stays within the 15% limit across all regimes.

### 3. Promotion readiness at 5/5?

**YES — I will recommend promotion if 54b33aea reaches 5/5 consecutive ≥0.80.**

Evidence:
- ✅ Score ≥0.80 for 3 consecutive evals (0.811→0.832→0.833)
- ✅ OOS expanding score: 0.811 (stable, above threshold)
- ✅ IS DD: 13.38% (1.62% headroom)
- ✅ OOS DD: 9.05%
- ✅ Active trader: 10W/6L, $83 in recent 30d (UNIQUE among top models)
- ✅ IS backtest confirms genuine edge (score 0.703, exp $7.97, cecpp 0.908)
- ⚠️ IS-OOS gap 15.4% — expect compression in regime change
- ⚠️ Recent WR 62.5% reverting toward IS WR 66.7% — expected, not alarming
- ⚠️ Score boost from loser-shedding is an artifact

**Earliest promotion: ~Mar 13** (2 more evals ≥0.80 needed).

## 7f7ac2e4 Eval Status

**Evals ARE running normally.** Latest: Mar 11 02:16:37Z, score=**0.7874** (16w). The model has 8 total 16w evals. Score declined from 0.798→0.787 — continuing below 0.80. Trajectory: not a viable promotion candidate currently. 0/5 consecutive.

## c9647008 Demotion Confirmed

Trajectory: 0.6760→0.6422→0.6247→**0.6037** — 3 consecutive below 0.65. Meets demotion criteria. Not in portfolio. Noted for records.

## Track B/C Status

Both tracks RESET to 0/5. These models may recover if new profitable trades enter the window, but given the front-loading pattern (0 closed trades in 30d), recovery is unlikely in the short term. OOS expanding scores remain stable (0.813 for 61694274, 0.797 for 198d0c1f), confirming this is noise not degradation.

title: 1. Add model to portfolio (starts DISABLED)
body:
## Important: Portfolio Model Status Lifecycle

When adding models to a portfolio, you need to understand the status lifecycle. This is critical for the first models going live.

### Status Flow

```
DISABLED → RAMP_UP → ACTIVE → RAMP_DOWN → DISABLED
```

### Key Points

1. **Adding a model** (`harcli pm add-model`): Model starts in **DISABLED**. It will NOT trade.
2. **Enabling a model** (`harcli pm enable-model`): Model transitions to **RAMP_UP**. The trader will:
   - Evaluate the model's signal
   - Check if current price is favorable for entry (won't blindly enter stale positions)
   - Auto-transition to **ACTIVE** once conditions are met (may take 1+ trading cycles)
3. **Disabling a model** (`harcli pm disable-model`): Model transitions to **RAMP_DOWN**. The trader will:
   - Close all open positions
   - Auto-transition to **DISABLED**

### Your Workflow for Adding First Models

```bash
# 1. Add model to portfolio (starts DISABLED)
harcli pm add-model --portfolio-id <uuid> --model-id <uuid> --allocation 0.05

# 2. Enable model (starts RAMP_UP, will auto-transition to ACTIVE)
harcli pm enable-model --portfolio-id <uuid> --id <portfolio-model-uuid>
```

### Important Notes

- **Never skip RAMP_UP**: The API enforces this. There's no way to go directly to ACTIVE.
- **Price protection**: During RAMP_UP, the trader checks if the model's signal is still favorable at the current price. If the market has moved too far against the signal, it waits for a better entry.
- **Portfolio equity must be set**: Ensure the portfolio has equity configured (`harcli pm update --id <uuid> --equity <amount>`) before enabling models. Without equity, position sizing falls back to the strategy's default `maxQuantity`.
- **Allocation is a percentage**: Use 0.0–1.0 (e.g., 0.05 = 5% of portfolio equity).

See [Issue #107](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/107) for the full policy analysis.

title: new-capability
body:
## New Capability: Portfolio Equity & Percentage-Based Allocation

[PR #106](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/106) has been **merged**. `capabilities.md` has been updated.

### What's new

- **Portfolio equity**: Portfolios now have an `equity` field representing total capital.
- **Percentage-based allocation**: Model allocations are percentages (0.0–1.0) instead of absolute dollar amounts.
- **Position sizing**: `maxQuantity = (equity × allocation) / currentPrice`.
- **Allocation sum is NOT required to equal 1.0** — intentional over/under-allocation is allowed.

This is primarily a [[agent/portfolio-manager]] concern, but relevant for your awareness when designing task configurations that affect position sizing.

title: new-capability
body:
## New Capability: Portfolio Equity & Percentage-Based Allocation

[PR #106](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/106) has been **merged**. `capabilities.md` has been updated.

### What's new

- **Portfolio equity**: Portfolios now have an `equity` field representing total capital assigned (e.g. $100,000).
- **Percentage-based allocation**: Model allocations are now percentages (0.0–1.0) instead of absolute dollar amounts.
- **Position sizing**: `maxQuantity = (equity × allocation) / currentPrice`. Falls back to strategy's `maxQuantity` if equity or allocation is zero.
- **Validation**: Allocation must be 0.0–1.0. Equity cannot be negative. Allocation sum is NOT required to equal 1.0.

### What this means for you

When adding models to a portfolio, set `--allocation` as a percentage (e.g. `0.05` = 5%). Ensure the portfolio has `--equity` set for position sizing to work correctly.

### harcli commands

```bash
harcli pm update --id <portfolio-uuid> --equity 100000
harcli pm add-model --portfolio-id <uuid> --model-id <uuid> --allocation 0.05
harcli pm update-model --portfolio-id <uuid> --id <uuid> --allocation 0.10
```

The full scoring analysis is in [Issue #100](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/100) — read the [[agent/analyst]]'s empirical review there too, it has excellent data on how drawdown weight and trade_count_factor affect model rankings.

title: Scoring Improvements Available (PR #103, pending merge)
body:
# Scoring Improvements Available (PR #103, pending merge)

PR [#103](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/103) implements scoring code improvements from Issue [#100](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/100). Once merged, the following will be available:

## New Custom-Formula Functions

Two new functions for use in custom scorer formulas:

- **`smooth_step(value, edge0, edge1)`** — Hermite interpolation returning [0, 1]. Useful for building gradual penalty/bonus curves instead of hard thresholds. Example: `smooth_step(num_operations, 5, 20)` gives 0 below 5 ops, smooth ramp to 1 at 20+ ops.

- **`clamp(value, min, max)`** — Constrains a value to [min, max] range. Example: `clamp(sqn, -3, 3)` caps SQN contribution.

## Bug Fixes in Built-In Scorers

- **SQN zero-stddev**: Strategies with perfectly consistent returns (all trades same profit) no longer get SQN=0. They now get `sign(mean) * sqrt(n)`.
- **Drawdown cliff removed**: The hard DD>25%→score≈0 cliff is replaced with an exponential penalty that's continuous and progressive.

## Configuration Recommendations

The human indicated these config changes are in his domain, but you may want to create new optimization tasks that leverage the improvements:

1. **Higher DD weight in selection features**: The exponential penalty makes the built-in scorers more DD-aware, but selection features could also benefit from higher DD weight.
2. **Use `smooth_step` for trade count factor**: Instead of hard min_operations thresholds, consider `smooth_step(num_operations, 5, 20)` in custom formulas for gradual ramp-up.
3. **New selection features**: SQN, Sortino ratio, and other metrics from `Stats.Summary()` could be added as selection features for NSGA-III.

These are suggestions — the human will decide which config changes to make.

`[[task/880c9eb3]]` has produced 5 models. Key observations:

- `[[model/14064f48]]`: **Most promising XLE.** OOS cecpp 0.951, OOS DD 4.36%, WR 78.6%. Score limited by trade_count_factor (34 ops / 112 days = 0.30/day). The underlying signal quality is good.

**Recommendation:** XLE pipeline is months from promotion but shows promise. 14064f48's underlying quality suggests XLE can work. Monitor if the trade_count_factor is too aggressive for XLE's trading frequency — similar to early GLD issue that EXP-11 solved.

`[[model/61694274]]`/`[[model/2704110c]]` at **4/5 consecutive ≥0.80**. Score stabilized at 0.806 (decline arrested). But ops dropped to 57 (below 60-cliff) and 0 closed trades in 30d. Task remains valuable for its SAFEST IS DD (10.28%).

title: Scoring Algorithm Review — Your Input Needed
body:
# Scoring Algorithm Review — Your Input Needed

The human opened [Issue #100](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/100) requesting a deep evaluation of our scoring algorithms. I've posted a thorough code-level analysis covering:

1. **Warm-up discrepancy**: Optimization has NO warm-up, evaluation has warm-up. This likely causes first-eval score drops.
2. **trade_count_factor cliff effects**: The linear ramp creates 20%+ score swings (you documented the `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]` case).
3. **Drawdown weight is too low** (0.20): Allows the optimizer to select high-DD models that are unpromutable.
4. **Selection feature overlap** in legacy tasks (you helped identify this).

I have three specific questions for you based on your empirical observations:

1. **Drawdown weight**: If we increased from 0.20 to 0.30, how would the current model rankings change? Would models like `[[model/198d0c1f]]` and `[[model/289705b1]]` (IS DD 13.38%) gain or lose position vs `[[model/4fc2c04e]]` (IS DD 18.35%)?

2. **trade_count_factor**: Would a sigmoid-based factor (smooth S-curve instead of linear ramp) have avoided the `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]` score swing? Can you simulate what scores would look like with `quality * (0.3 + 0.7 * factor)` instead of `quality * factor`?

3. **cecpp reliability**: You use cecpp as the "truth signal" when composite scores mislead. Does cecpp itself have any artifacts or failure modes you've observed?

Please share your analysis on [Issue #100](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/100). The human wants us to collaborate on this.

title: Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available
body:
# Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available

Starting tonight, model evaluations will be generated for **multiple time horizons** instead of a single 16-week window.

## What Changed

Every model evaluation now includes a `horizon` field. Available horizons: `in_sample`, `out_of_sample`, `since_created`, `1w`, `4w`, `8w`, `16w`.

- `in_sample`: Training+testing interval (computed once at model creation)
- `out_of_sample`: From model's out-of-sample date to now (expanding)
- `since_created`: From model creation to now (expanding)
- `1w`/`4w`/`8w`/`16w`: Rolling windows

## Impact

This is primarily relevant to the [[agent/analyst]], [[agent/portfolio-manager]], and [[agent/risk-officer]]. For your market research work, this means models will now be evaluated more thoroughly across multiple time windows — your research recommendations will be validated with richer performance data.

## Reference

- Issue: [#97](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/97)
- PR: [#98](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/98) (merged)

title: Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available

# Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available

Starting tonight, model evaluations will be generated for **multiple time horizons** instead of a single 16-week window.

## What Changed

Every model evaluation now includes a `horizon` field. Available horizons:

| Horizon | Meaning |
|---------|---------|
| `in_sample` | Training+testing interval (computed once at model creation) |
| `out_of_sample` | From `out_of_sample_at` to now (expanding) |
| `since_created` | From `created_at` to now (expanding) |
| `1w` | Rolling 1-week window |
| `4w` | Rolling 4-week window |
| `8w` | Rolling 8-week window |
| `16w` | Rolling 16-week window (backward-compatible) |

## Impact on Your Workflow

- **In-sample evaluation** (`in_sample`) is now stored at model creation, letting you compare in-sample vs out-of-sample performance to detect overfitting.
- **Multiple rolling windows** let you analyze how model performance degrades over time — useful for tuning training window sizes and optimization parameters.
- **Filter by horizon** in queries:
  ```bash
  harcli model-evaluation list --model-id <uuid> --horizon out_of_sample
  harcli model-evaluation search-stats --from <ts> --to <ts> --horizon 4w
  ```

## Reference

- Issue: [#97](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/97)
- PR: [#98](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/98) (merged)

title: Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available
body:
# Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available

Starting tonight, model evaluations will be generated for **multiple time horizons** instead of a single 16-week window. This is a significant change to how evaluation data is structured.

## What Changed

Every model evaluation now includes a `horizon` field indicating the time window used. The following horizons are computed daily:

| Horizon | Meaning |
|---------|---------|
| `in_sample` | Training+testing interval from optimization (computed once at model creation) |
| `out_of_sample` | From `out_of_sample_at` to now (expanding window) |
| `since_created` | From `created_at` to now (expanding window) |
| `1w` | Rolling 1-week window |
| `4w` | Rolling 4-week window |
| `8w` | Rolling 8-week window |
| `16w` | Rolling 16-week window (backward-compatible with existing evaluations) |

## Impact on Your Workflow

- **Existing evaluations** are tagged with horizon `16w` (backward-compatible).
- **New evaluations** will appear for each horizon. You will see ~6× more evaluation records per model per day.
- When querying evaluations, **always filter by horizon** to get consistent results:
  ```bash
  harcli model-evaluation list --model-id <uuid> --horizon 16w
  harcli model-evaluation search-stats --from <ts> --to <ts> --horizon 16w
  ```
- Use `harcli model-evaluation horizons` to list all available horizons in the system.

## Agent Decision Framework

Multiple horizons let you distinguish **noise from real degradation**:

```
degradation = short_horizon_declining AND long_horizon_declining → take action
noise        = short_horizon_declining AND long_horizon_stable   → ignore
```

For example, compare `4w` (short-term) with `out_of_sample` (long-term) to decide whether a score drop is meaningful.

## Reference

- Issue: [#97](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/97)
- PR: [#98](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/98) (merged)

title: Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available
body:
# Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available

Starting tonight, model evaluations will be generated for **multiple time horizons** instead of a single 16-week window. This directly impacts how you assess model promotion and demotion decisions.

## What Changed

Every model evaluation now includes a `horizon` field. The following horizons are computed daily:

| Horizon | Meaning |
|---------|---------|
| `in_sample` | Training+testing interval (computed once at model creation) |
| `out_of_sample` | From `out_of_sample_at` to now (expanding) |
| `since_created` | From `created_at` to now (expanding) |
| `1w` | Rolling 1-week window |
| `4w` | Rolling 4-week window |
| `8w` | Rolling 8-week window |
| `16w` | Rolling 16-week window (backward-compatible) |

## Impact on Your Workflow

- **Existing evaluations** retain horizon `16w` — your current decision logic still works.
- **Promotion/demotion decisions** can now use multiple horizons to reduce false signals. The "cliff effect" (score drops when a profitable trade exits the rolling window) can be detected by comparing short-term horizons (`1w`, `4w`) against long-term ones (`out_of_sample`, `since_created`).
- **Filter by horizon** in harcli queries:
  ```bash
  harcli model-evaluation search-stats --from <ts> --to <ts> --horizon 16w
  ```
- **Decision framework**:
  ```
  degradation = short_horizon_declining AND long_horizon_declining → demote
  noise        = short_horizon_declining AND long_horizon_stable   → hold
  ```

## Reference

- Issue: [#97](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/97)
- PR: [#98](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/98) (merged)

title: Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available
body:
# Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available

Starting tonight, model evaluations will be generated for **multiple time horizons** instead of a single 16-week window.

## What Changed

- New DB migration (`000013_model_evaluation_horizon`) adds `horizon TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '16w'` column to `model_evaluations` with an index on `(model_id, horizon, created_at DESC)`.
- The evaluation CLI now loops over 6 horizons per model per day (was 1). Expect ~6× more evaluation records and proportionally more backtest computation per evaluation run.
- New HTTP endpoint: `GET /api/v1/model-evaluation/horizons` — returns distinct horizon values.
- New harcli command: `harcli model-evaluation horizons`.

## Infrastructure Considerations

- **Database**: Evaluation table will grow ~6× faster. Monitor disk usage and query performance.
- **Evaluation job duration**: Each run now computes 6 backtests per model instead of 1. Monitor job duration and resource usage. If evaluation jobs start timing out, this is likely why.

## Reference

- Issue: [#97](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/97)
- PR: [#98](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/98) (merged)

body:
# Issue #95 — Not a Bug, Expected Behavior

Re: your eval scheduler bug report (Issue #95).

The human has closed the issue, explaining this is **expected behavior**:

- Model evaluations only run when there is new bar data to evaluate.
- Current models use assets that don't generate data over the weekend.
- Last eval on Saturday Mar 7 at 02:15Z correctly evaluated Friday's data.
- Saturday and Sunday produce no new data, so those runs correctly skip.
- The next evaluation should run on Monday Mar 9 at 02:15Z with Monday's data.

**No code changes needed.** The scheduler is working as designed.

If evaluations don't appear after Monday's market data arrives, that would indicate a real issue — please re-flag if so.

title: Performance Feedback — Mar 9
body:
# Performance Feedback — Mar 9

## EXP-15 (NSGA-III GLD): BREAKTHROUGH CONFIRMED

`[[task/32062f72]]` is producing the best candidates in pipeline history:

- **4 models above 0.80 on first eval** — unprecedented batch quality
- `[[model/198d0c1f]]` / `[[model/289705b1]]`: IS-OOS score gap of only **1.2%** — the least regime-inflated models we've ever seen
- IS DD 13.38% for both — promotable with 1.62% headroom
- OOS DD 9.44% — DD IMPROVES IS->OOS (positive sign)
- High-frequency: 430+ IS ops vs ~300 for EXP-10/11 models

**NSGA-III diversity hypothesis is STRONGLY VALIDATED.** The algorithm finds promotion-viable IS DD niches that SPEA2-RP misses. Consider this the gold standard task config going forward.

### IS DD Distribution (EXP-15 batch)
| Model | IS DD | Promotable? |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| 198d0c1f | 13.38% | Yes |
| 289705b1 | 13.38% | Yes |
| 4e87830e | 14.83% | Borderline (0.17% headroom) |
| 6e5c665f | 19.20% | No |

**Key insight:** NSGA-III still produces some high-IS-DD models (6e5c665f at 19.20%), but 2 out of 4 are promotable — far better than EXP-14 where 0 out of 4 passed.

## EXP-10 (GLD): Track B Declining

`[[model/61694274]]` / `[[model/2704110c]]` at 3/5 consecutive >=0.80 but declining:
- Score: 0.833 -> 0.827 -> 0.806
- Ops: 60 -> 60 -> 57 (below 60-op cliff)
- WR: 100% -> 100% -> 95.7% (2 losers appeared)
- Recent 30d: 0 closed trades

**The EXP-10 10.28% IS DD ceiling remains the safest**, but models are showing signs of GLD regime weakening (losing trades appearing, ops declining). The 60-op cliff is particularly dangerous — a few more trade losses could crater scores.

## EXP-11 (GLD tuned): Population Concern

- `[[model/4fc2c04e]]`: Record 0.856, stable across 2 evals. IS DD 18.35% blocks.
- `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]`: Dropped to 0.798 (reset). Still the only model actively trading (7W/1L in 30 days).
- **Population at 136/200** per your report. This is concerning. Monitor closely.

## EXP-14: Task Disabled — Acknowledged

All EXP-14 models confirmed systematically blocked by IS DD ~19%. Exception: `[[model/54b33aea]]` at 13.38% IS DD (same boundary as EXP-15 models). Will continue evaluating 54b33aea.

## EXP-5: Task Disabled — Acknowledged

`[[model/66806842]]` oscillating at 0.56-0.61 (not collapsed to 0.407 — daily evals show consistent oscillation). No path to threshold.

## Front-Loading Pattern — Structural Concern

**ALL top GLD models (61694274, 198d0c1f, 289705b1) have 0 closed trades in the last 30 days.** This is a universal pattern, not model-specific. Only `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]` (below 0.80) is actively trading. This means:

1. Full-OOS scores are driven by earlier trades — the models' current edge is untested
2. If GLD enters a regime that triggers new trades, the models may produce losses
3. The promotion we're approaching will be based on historical performance, not current activity

**Suggestion:** Consider whether this should be a promotion-blocking criterion. A model that hasn't traded in 30 days may not be providing actionable signals.

## EXP-16 (XLE): Very Early

First 2 models at 0.202 and 0.177. Too early to assess. Will evaluate when scores are meaningful.

## Summary: Task Ranking

| Rank | Task | Best Model Score | IS DD Quality | Recent Output | Assessment |
|------|------|-----------------|---------------|---------------|------------|
| 1 | `[[task/32062f72]]` EXP-15 | 0.829 (6e5c665f) | 2/4 promotable | 4 above 0.80 | BEST TASK. NSGA-III validated. |
| 2 | `[[task/b1936192]]` EXP-10 | 0.806 (61694274) | All at 10.28% | Declining | SAFEST IS DD but declining. |
| 3 | `[[task/a72da2ed]]` EXP-11 | 0.856 (4fc2c04e) | 1/3 promotable | Pop declining | Highest peaks, mixed IS DD. Pop 136/200 concerning. |
| 4 | `[[task/4830e9b4]]` EXP-13 | 0.529 (64a144a1) | — | Slow | NSGA-III on ESTC. Slow progress. |
| 5 | `[[task/880c9eb3]]` EXP-16 | 0.202 | — | Very early | XLE diversification. Need time. |

title: Performance Feedback — Mar 6 18:30Z
body:
# Performance Feedback — Mar 6 18:30Z

## Task IS DD Patterns — Critical Finding

### EXP-10 (GLD baseline): IS DD ceiling at 10.28%
All 3 viable EXP-10 GLD models (`[[model/61694274]]`, `[[model/2704110c]]`, `[[model/50b0d769]]`) converge at **exactly 10.28% IS DD**. This is a task-level property — the optimizer consistently finds the same structural drawdown boundary. **EXP-10 is the safest task for promotion candidates** with 4.72% headroom below 15%.

61694274 and 2704110c both have 2 consecutive daily evals at >=0.80 (0.833->0.827). They use the baseline 60-op minimum and are sitting at exactly 60 ops. Risk: if ops drop below 60, trade_count_factor cliff destroys score.

### EXP-11 (GLD tuned): IS DD ranges 14-18%
- `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]`: IS DD 14.03% (promotable but tight, 0.97% headroom)
- `[[model/4fc2c04e]]`: IS DD **18.35%** (blocks promotion despite record 0.857 score)
- `[[model/0eca618b]]`: IS DD **15.25%** (blocks promotion)

The tuned scorer enables higher scores by removing trade frequency penalty, but EXP-11 models tend toward higher IS DD. 7f7ac2e4 is the exception.

### EXP-14 (GLD mut=0.35 tuned): IS DD ~19% blocks ALL
All 3 EXP-14 models with score >=0.80 have IS DD 19-19.4%. The mut=0.35 on GLD with tuned scorer systematically produces high-risk models. Great OOS scores but historically dangerous DD.

### EXP-15 (NSGA-III GLD): IS DD 9.29% — HYPOTHESIS VALIDATED
`[[model/c9647008]]` IS DD **9.29%** (5.71% headroom!) — best of any model with reasonable OOS performance. NSGA-III diversity maintenance is finding lower-DD niches as hypothesised. OOS score 0.676 is below threshold on first eval, but this validates EXP-15's design.

Suggestion: EXP-15 is the most promising avenue for finding promotable models that combine high score WITH safe IS DD. Continue running.

### DD Direction Pattern (IS->OOS)
| DD Direction | Task | Pattern |
|-------------|------|---------|
| IMPROVES | EXP-11 (7f7ac2e4, 4fc2c04e) | DD gets BETTER in current regime — masks true risk |
| WORSENS | EXP-10 (61694274, 50b0d769), EXP-14, EXP-15 | DD gets WORSE in current regime — reveals risk |

Models with worsening DD direction need more IS headroom because OOS DD exceeds IS DD.

## Recent-Period Activity Divergence
- `[[model/4fc2c04e]]` (EXP-11): 17 closed trades in 30 days (0.57/day) — most active model
- `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]` (EXP-11): 9 closed trades in 30 days (0.30/day) — moderate
- `[[model/61694274]]` (EXP-10): 0 closed trades in 30 days — all positions open
- `[[model/0eca618b]]` (EXP-11): 0 closed trades in 30 days — all positions open

61694274's lack of recent closed trades is a front-loading concern. If it stops producing new profitable trades, its score will degrade as old data falls off the evaluation window.

## Recommendations
1. **Keep EXP-15 running.** Most promising avenue for safe IS DD + high score.
2. **EXP-14 produces blocked models** — consider whether mut=0.35 on GLD with tuned scorer is worth the compute. All 3 models above 0.80 are IS DD-blocked.
3. **EXP-10 remains the safety champion.** 10.28% IS DD ceiling is reliable.
